findings.description: Textbook N+1: monthly_revenue_report fires one order_items query per paid order inside the loop (line 112 inside the loop opened at 111) and aggregates row-by-row in Python; a month with 10k paid orders means 10,001 round-trips when a single JOIN + GROUP BY (or one SUM aggregate) would do it in one query.

Non-sargable date filter: wrapping the indexed paid_at column in strftime('%Y', paid_at)=? AND strftime('%m', paid_at)=? forces a full table scan of orders every report (any index on paid_at is unusable); a range predicate (paid_at >= start AND paid_at < next_month) stays index-friendly.

No connection pooling/reuse: _connect opens a brand-new sqlite3.connect on every public call (charge_order/refund/report), paying connection + PRAGMA setup per web request instead of reusing a pooled/long-lived connection.
SELECT * on orders in charge_order when only discount_pct (plus row existence) is consumed; over-fetches every column including potentially large blobs/PII per charge.
Out of perf scope, flagging for critic-security: logger.info writes the full card_number and cvv in cleartext to the payments log (PCI-DSS violation); deferring detail to the security critic.
